name: Upside-Down Pineapple-Raisin-Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the pineapple and reserve half a cup of juice.
2. Melt one-fourth cup margarine in a 10-inch ovenproof skillet.
3. Blend the brown sugar into the melted margarine.
4. Arrange the sliced pineapple over the sugar mixture and top with half a cup of raisins.
5. Beat the remaining one-fourth cup margarine with granulated sugar until light and fluffy.
6. Add eggs, vanilla extract, and shredded carrots to the mixture and beat until combined.
7. Gradually mix in the dry ingredients (flour, salt, baking powder, baking soda, cinnamon, and ginger) into the creamed mixture.
8. Beat in half a cup of reserved pineapple juice until blended.
9. Repeat step 7, ending with the remaining dry ingredients.
10. Fold in the remaining half cup of raisins.
11. Pour the batter over the pineapple.
12. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for 40-45 minutes.
13. Let stand for 5 minutes before inverting onto a serving plate.
